nodes.name: 反嘴鹬属
nodes.summary: 反嘴鹬属（Recurvirostra）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联4种鸟类，如美洲反嘴鹬、Andean avocet、反嘴鹬等4种鸟类。

nodes.name: Andean avocet
nodes.summary: Andean avocet（Recurvirostra andina）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Andean avocet。

nodes.name: Andean avocet
nodes.summary: The Andean avocet (Recurvirostra andina) is a large wader in the avocet and stilt bird family, Recurvirostridae. It is resident in the Andes, breeding above 3500 m in northwestern Argentina, western Bolivia, northern Chile and southern Peru. Description This avocet has a white head, neck, underparts and rump, and dark brown back, wings and tail. It is similar in size but slightly bulkier than the American avocet, at 43–48 cm (17–19 in) in length and 315–410 g (11.1–14.5 oz) in weight. Average body mass is 361 g (12.7 oz), making it likely the heaviest of the Recurvirostridae family. The thin, grey legs are not as long as with other avocet species, but the long thin black bill is upturned at the end. The sexes are similar, and the juvenile plumage is undescribed. The colour pattern is similar to that of the local subspecies Himantopus himantopus melanurus of black-winged stilt, but that bird has very long red legs a white tail and a straight bill. Behaviour The Andean avocet nests near shallow, preferably alkaline lakes in the Andes, often in small groups. The eggs are laid in at least January. This species is non-migratory, but may move to slightly lower altitudes when not breeding. The Andean avocet forages in shallow water or on mud flats, often sweeping its bill from side to side in water as it seeks its crustacean and insect prey. References Hayman, Marchant and Prater, Shorebirds ISBN 0-395-37903-2
